title: vicia acerosa var. angustifolia
intro: Vicia acerosa var. angustifolia, a lesser-known species of the vetch family (Fabaceae), is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, Vicia acerosa var. angustifolia is typically used as a dried herb, infusion, or decoction, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
